$MU 
 
This is more sentiment noise than anything real. 
 
When a crowded name like Micron gets attention from big public commentary, you often get short-term volatility — not because the story changed, but because positioning shifts. 
 
Either dip gets bought fast, or it flushes a bit more before stabilizing. 
 
Nothing changes in the business. Just market behavior.

$MU is still up about 248% YTD. Let’s not turn every pullback into a 5-alarm fire just yet. 
 
Perspective matters more than emotion in markets like this. When a name has already moved that far that fast, volatility is not a surprise — it’s part of the digestion process. 
 
Zooming out helps. Short-term swings feel big, but structurally nothing changes unless key levels actually break with confirmation. 
 
Looking to focus on the names setting up in strong groups.

Today:

$JACK
Some more math:

Only ~19.1M shares outstanding.
~6.7M shares are officially short.
Official short float is ~35.7%.
Days to cover is 10.6.

Now the key part people miss: if around 1M shares are locked by Russell/iShares/index-style holders this week, that means roughly 1M fewer shares actually trading freely.

So shorts are not fighting 19M easy shares. They could be fighting a much smaller real tradable float.

Today's selloff across AI compute and semiconductor stocks is another reminder that market sentiment often moves faster than fundamentals. 
 
If even UBS says $META 's compute strategy isn't new, this looks more like a headline-driven repricing than a change in the long-term AI story. Stocks like $AEHR , $AAOI , SIVE, $SNDK , MRVL, LITE, and $NBIS  sold off sharply, but the long-term demand for AI infrastructure, optical networking, memory, and compute hasn't disappeared. 
 
I&#3

What a volatile week across the board. 
 
$SPX managed to finish the session green, but $QQQ lagged hard, closing down ~1.7% -clear sign of tech and semis weakness dragging into the long weekend. 
 
We’re seeing rotation under the surface: broad index resilience on one side, while growth and chip names are showing fatigue on the other. 
 
This is not a clean trend environment anymore -it’s selective, headline-driven, and increasingly two-speed. 
 
Next week is the real tell for July positioning.

posts.body:
Google announced plans to build a new data center in Michigan in partnership with DTE Energy, committing to enable 2.7 GW of new clean energy to support the local grid. 
The project, under evaluation in Van Buren Township, will add 24/7 clean power—including solar, advanced storage, and demand flexibility—through a Clean Capacity Acceleration Agreement, supporting the state’s transition away from coal while improving grid reliability.

posts.body:
AI compute demand keeps stretching higher. 20GW in 2026, ~30GW in 2027 even with delays. Frontier AI may need 100GW+ by 2030. 
 
Compute stays scarce, pricing stays firm, and margins at the model layer support aggressive bidding for capacity. 
 
That’s the setup for powered sites, AI data centers, and GPU infra. If demand keeps outrunning supply, $IREN benefits from higher utilization and better long-term revenue.

posts.body:

Stock is up ~6% AH following news of a $22.3M pre-payment from $COHR tied to a 3-year agreement. That kind of upfront capital commitment is not just a headline -it improves visibility on cash flow and validates demand strength in the underlying supply chain. 
 
Market reacting to the derisking effect here: better near-term liquidity + stronger customer conviction over multi-year demand. 
 
Short-term move is about flow, but longer-term it’s ab

posts.title: $AXTI Coherent ($COHR) does not produce raw Indium Phosphide (InP) substrate wafers. The company internally grows materials like Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) and Silicon Carbide (SiC), but relies on extern
posts.body: $AXTI Coherent ($COHR) does not produce raw Indium Phosphide (InP) substrate wafers. The company internally grows materials like Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) and Silicon Carbide (SiC), but relies on external vendors for raw, blank InP wafers. Coherent purchases these substrates through long-term supply agreements.Once Coherent procures the raw InP wafers from outside suppliers, they perform internal epitaxy and device fabrication. Coherent is an industry leader in manufacturing high-volume InP-based

SPY.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
SPY.blurb: Mixed sentiment (13 vs 11); users are discussing broader market trends and gratitude.

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.blurb: Bullish (14 vs 4); users expect $420 next week and see it as a millionaire maker.

META.name: Meta Platforms (Facebook)
META.blurb: Slightly bearish (12 vs 14); some warn of capex cuts that could hurt AI stocks.

WEN.name: Wendy’s Company
WEN.blurb: Extremely bullish (39 vs 3); meme-like enthusiasm with users targeting $10.

SNDK.blurb: Split sentiment (15 vs 17); some bullish alongside MU, others call it 'scamdisk.'

NBIS.blurb: Bullish (20 vs 7); users see it reaching 350 or 260 next week.

QQQ.blurb: Neutral (10 vs 8); discussions about long-only bull funds and gold vs Nasdaq.

DTE.blurb: Unanimously bullish (12 vs 0); users pair it with growth and income stocks.

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.blurb: Neutral (8 vs 7); users expect a pre-market drop but are watching options.

IREN.blurb: Bullish (15 vs 8); some users are heartbroken, others expect a comeback July 6th.

sentiment.reasoning: The overall mood leans bullish, driven by strong positive sentiment on top-mentioned stocks like MU, NVDA, and TSLA, where bullish tags far outnumber bearish. However, mixed signals on META and SNDK, plus bearish tags on AXTI, keep confidence from being higher.

themes.title: Semiconductor surge continues
themes.explanation: MU leads Reddit mentions with 1167, up from 936, and Stocktwits sentiment is heavily bullish (24 bullish vs 3 bearish). SNDK also spiked from 127 to 262 mentions. The community sees the recent drop as a buying opportunity, with messages like 'Take advantage of the drop' and 'we are no where near the top.' This suggests strong conviction in the chip sector.

themes.title: AI and tech mega-caps in focus
themes.explanation: NVDA, MSFT, and META remain top of mind. NVDA has 22 bullish vs 6 bearish tags, and MSFT 14 vs 4. However, META shows a split (12 bullish, 14 bearish) with some users warning of capex cuts. The AI narrative is still strong, but there's debate about sustainability.

themes.title: Wendy's meme-like enthusiasm
themes.explanation: WEN has an extremely bullish sentiment (39 bullish, 3 bearish) despite being a fast-food stock. Users are posting about 'Dave’s double + Frosty' and targeting $10. This looks like a meme-driven rally, not fundamentals, so it's high risk.

themes.title: Tesla and Apple see renewed interest
themes.explanation: TSLA mentions jumped from 75 to 163, and AAPL from 12 to 101. TSLA sentiment is very bullish (15 vs 2), with one user predicting '$TSLA back over $400 Monday.' AAPL sentiment is neutral (8 vs 7), but the spike in mentions suggests anticipation around upcoming events.

themes.title: Energy and utilities get attention
themes.explanation: DTE Energy mentions rose from 67 to 103, with 12 bullish and 0 bearish tags. EU (enCore Energy) jumped from 15 to 78 mentions. This could reflect a rotation into defensive or energy plays amid market uncertainty.

themes.title: Leveraged ETFs attract traders
themes.explanation: SOXL, a 3x semiconductor bull ETF, saw mentions rise from 24 to 74. This indicates retail traders are using leverage to bet on chip recovery. It's a high-risk play that amplifies both gains and losses.

tickers_to_watch.reason: MU is the most mentioned stock on Reddit with 1167 mentions, up 25% from yesterday. Stocktwits sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish (24 vs 3). Users are calling the recent drop a buying opportunity and predicting a return to $1100+. The strong community conviction could drive short-term momentum.

SNDK mentions doubled from 127 to 262, but Stocktwits sentiment is split (15 bullish, 17 bearish). Some users are bullish alongside MU, while others call it 'scamdisk' and predict -62%. The mixed signals suggest high uncertainty, so watch for a breakout direction.

TSLA mentions more than doubled to 163, and sentiment is very bullish (15 vs 2). One popular message predicts a return to $400 next week. The community seems to be ignoring broader market concerns and focusing on Tesla's AI and EV leadership.

WEN has an extremely bullish sentiment (39 vs 3) despite being a fast-food stock. Users are posting about eating Wendy's and targeting $10. This looks like a meme stock revival, so it could spike quickly but also crash just as fast.

DTE mentions rose 54% to 103, and sentiment is unanimously bullish (12 vs 0). Users are pairing it with other growth and income stocks. This could be a defensive play as investors seek stable dividends amid tech volatility.

risk_factors.title: Meme stock mania in WEN
risk_factors.explanation: Wendy's has no major catalyst, yet it has 39 bullish tags vs 3 bearish. This looks like a coordinated pump, which can reverse violently. Regular investors should be cautious about chasing hype without fundamentals.

risk_factors.title: META bearish undercurrent
risk_factors.explanation: Despite high mentions, META has more bearish than bullish tags (14 vs 12). One message warns of a 'capex cut coming soon announcement tomorrow.' If Meta cuts AI spending, it could drag down the entire AI sector.

risk_factors.title: Leveraged ETF risk in SOXL
risk_factors.explanation: SOXL mentions surged from 24 to 74, indicating retail traders are using 3x leverage to bet on semiconductors. These ETFs decay over time and can lead to massive losses if the market moves against them.

risk_factors.title: AXTI bearish sentiment
risk_factors.explanation: AXTI has 9 bullish vs 22 bearish tags, the most bearish in the data. Users are calling it a 'buy' in some messages but also linking it to 'scamdisk.' The negative sentiment could weigh on the stock.

contrarian_views.title: Semiconductor top is near
contrarian_views.explanation: While most are bullish on MU and SNDK, some users argue 'we are no where near the top' sarcastically, implying the opposite. The rapid rise in mentions and leveraged ETF interest could signal a crowded trade that is due for a pullback.

contrarian_views.title: META capex cuts could hurt AI
contrarian_views.explanation: A popular message warns that Meta will announce capex cuts, which would be bearish for AI infrastructure stocks. If Meta reduces spending, it could trigger a revaluation of the entire AI trade, affecting NVDA, MU, and others.

contrarian_views.title: Market correction trap
contrarian_views.explanation: One user calls the selloff a 'correction trap' with 70% probability of a rebound, but this optimism could be misplaced. The contrarian view is that the market is due for a deeper correction, especially if earnings disappoint.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is the semiconductor surge, led by Micron (MU) with 1,167 Reddit mentions, up from 936 yesterday. Stocktwits sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish (24 bullish vs 3 bearish), with users calling the recent drop a buying opportunity and predicting a return to $1,100+. Sandisk (SNDK) also saw mentions double to 262, though sentiment is split (15 vs 17). The community is convinced that AI demand will keep driving chip stocks higher, but some skeptics warn that the rapid rise in leveraged ETF interest (SOXL mentions tripled to 74) signals a crowded trade.

Other notable discussions include Tesla (TSLA) and Apple (AAPL), which saw mention counts more than double. TSLA has 15 bullish vs 2 bearish tags, with one user predicting a return to $400 next week. Apple's sentiment is neutral (8 vs 7), but the spike in mentions suggests anticipation around upcoming product launches. Meanwhile, Wendy's (WEN) continues to attract meme-like enthusiasm with 39 bullish tags, despite being a fast-food stock. Users are posting about eating Wendy's and targeting $10, which looks like a coordinated pump rather than fundamentals.

The crowd is divided on several fronts. META has more bearish than bullish tags (14 vs 12), with one message warning of a 'capex cut coming soon announcement tomorrow' that could drag down the AI sector. AXTI is the most bearish stock in the data (9 vs 22), with users calling it a 'buy' but also linking it to 'scamdisk.' On the other hand, DTE Energy has unanimous bullish sentiment (12 vs 0), suggesting a rotation into defensive plays. This tension between tech optimism and defensive caution suggests the market is uncertain about the near-term direction.

overlooked_insights.title: Micron's NY plant pipeline and nuclear hydrogen deal
overlooked_insights.insight: Micron's under-construction facility in Clay, NY is fed by a 55-inch pipeline from Lake Ontario and has a partnership with Constellation Energy's Nine Mile Point nuclear plant for power and hydrogen supply. This level of infrastructure detail is absent from mainstream coverage.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user posted: '$MU the Micron Plant they are building in Clay, NY has a 55" pipeline to it from Lake Ontario. Constellation Energy has a nuclear power plant - 9 mile point in Oswego that is partnering with Micron to provide them energy including Hydrogen.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream headlines focus on Micron's AI contract optimism and stock moves, not the physical buildout and energy sourcing.

overlooked_insights.title: Michael Burry shorting MU at $1052
overlooked_insights.insight: Multiple users claim Michael Burry opened a short position on Micron near $1052 per share. This specific entry price is not reported in mainstream articles about Burry's latest short.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user wrote: '$QQQ $SPY $TSLA $NVDA Michael Burry is short Micron $MU from $1052.' Another replied: '$MU ya right. Burry destroyed $PLTR and $NVDA MU IS NEXT.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream news notes Burry is short Micron and AI names but omits the precise price level he may have used.

overlooked_insights.title: META capex cut announcement rumored for tomorrow
overlooked_insights.insight: A user predicts Meta will announce a capital expenditure cut tomorrow, directly countering the narrative that Meta is increasing AI infrastructure spending. If true, it could reverse the selloff in AI infrastructure stocks.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user stated: '$META capex cut coming soon announcement tomorrow' (tagged bullish). Another user argued spending may accelerate in 2027.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage emphasizes Meta's pivot to a cloud compute business and higher capex, not a near-term cut.

overlooked_insights.title: IREN CEO $800M RSU grant with lockup and manipulation claims
overlooked_insights.insight: Users detail that IREN's co-CEOs received $800 million in restricted stock (19 million shares) with a lockup until 2031, and claim the subsequent 50% selloff was engineered by market makers who loaded up on $40 puts on June 3rd.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user posted: '$IREN Two executives awarded themselves $800 million in compensation with a dilution of 19 million shares.' Another: 'MM's showed us their hands June 3rd... I noticed a massive number of PUTs with strike prices down to $40 bucks...'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream headlines cover the stock grant and the Warriors partnership but ignore the options positioning and the narrative of a manufactured drop.

overlooked_insights.title: AXTI-Coherent prepayment and question of U.S. facility
overlooked_insights.insight: AXT's subsidiary received a $22.3 million prepayment from Coherent under a three-year wafer supply agreement. A user questions whether this supply is for a U.S. facility, which could have significant geopolitical implications for domestic chip production.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user wrote: '$AXTI has signed a three-year wafer supply agreement with $COHR... prepayment of $22,288,500.' Another commented: 'Would be good to know if this is for a U.S. facility or one of their international sites. Could matter a lot for the optics group.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream reports the deal as a routine supply agreement without analyzing the location or its potential policy relevance.

overlooked_insights.title: Wendy's first Italy store opening and gamma squeeze pattern
overlooked_insights.insight: Users point to the imminent opening of Wendy's first Italian location in Milan and a weekly price pattern ($7.5→$8.5→$9.5) that they believe is building pressure for a gamma squeeze. Mainstream coverage dismisses the stock as a meme rally.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user noted: '$WEN Interesting. So the first Wendy's in Italy (Milan) should be opening any time now.' Another: '$WEN last week $7.5 This week $8.5 So next week $9.5 Slowly build up pressure for Gamma Squeeze.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines focus on the broader restaurant sector and Joe Libe's new CFO, ignoring the international expansion catalyst and the speculative squeeze thesis.

overlooked_insights.title: MU trades at 7x forward earnings, 6x below semis average
overlooked_insights.insight: A user highlights that Micron is trading under 7 times forward earnings, roughly six times cheaper than the semiconductor sector average, suggesting deep undervaluation not reflected in the stock's recent decline.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user stated: '$MU trades under 7x forward earnings. Almost 6 times less than the semis average. Its unbelievablely cheap.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream commentary focuses on the selloff being overdone in a general sense, but does not highlight this specific relative valuation metric.

overlooked_insights.title: Record $180B long-only bull fund inflows in late June 2026
overlooked_insights.insight: A user claims that equity inflows from long-only bull funds hit an all-time high of $180 billion in late June 2026, surpassing the previous peak during the 2021 meme/SPAC bubble. This macro data point is not cited in any mainstream headline.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user wrote: '$QQQ ~ Long only bull funds vs equities. June 2026. Equity inflows from long only bull funds were at all-time highs of about 180 billion in late June 2026 surpassing the legendary bubble mania peak of the {NFT/Meme Stocks/SPACs} mania.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream articles focus on sector rotation and the Mag 7 loss but do not provide this specific fund-flow figure.

catalysts.expected_impact: If true, Meta's stock could rally on reduced spending fears, while neocloud and infrastructure stocks (IREN, CRWV) could suffer further.
One user expects a broad sector reversal on July 6, which if realized could lead to a sharp bounce in IREN and other beaten-down names.
International expansion news could reignite retail interest and support the meme/ squeeze narrative.
Earnings will provide clarity on the financial impact of the Coherent supply agreement and overall demand trends.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Meta's AI agent development is slower than expected, and its cloud compute pivot is causing a sector-wide selloff.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Some users believe Meta will announce a capex cut (bullish for Meta but bearish for infrastructure), and that the selloff is overdone for other names like MU.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd is split; the 'capex cut tomorrow' claim lacks verification. The news narrative is more consistent with the actual price action, but the crowd's focus on Meta's internal cost structure is a valid potential driver that mainstream outlets are only partially covering.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Chip stocks tumble again on valuation concerns and Meta's cloud plan; Trump's comment fails to stop the decline.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Many users see the pullback as a buying opportunity, citing a forward P/E of 7x (6x below semis average) and Burry's short as a potential contrarian signal.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's valuation argument (7x forward earnings) is a concrete data point that justifies a contrarian view. The news narrative is short-term bearish, but the crowd's long-term bullish case has merit if the valuation is as extreme as claimed.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Wendy's is a meme stock rally without growth; investors are warned not to chase it.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Users are overwhelmingly bullish, citing international expansion (Italy), product innovation (Minions meal), and a technical pattern building for a gamma squeeze.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's focus on tangible developments like the Italy opening and new menu items gives their thesis more substance than a pure meme. However, the gamma squeeze pattern is speculative. The news narrative correctly underscores the risk, but may underestimate the impact of real operational catalysts.

MU.summary: Micron (MU) is getting a ton of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions surging 25% in the last 24 hours. The mood is overwhelmingly bullish—24 bullish tags vs just 3 bearish on Stocktwits. People are calling the recent dip a buying opportunity, with one user saying "take advantage of the drop" and another predicting a return to $1,100+ next week. The stock is still up about 248% year-to-date, so many see this pullback as normal volatility, not a trend reversal. Comparisons to SanDisk (SNDK) and other chip stocks are common, with some saying "we are no where near the top." There's a sense of confidence that AI demand will keep driving MU higher, though a few acknowledge it could chop around in the $1,000 range for weeks. One user joked that even 25 years from now people will still be calling AI a bubble. Overall, the community is excited but aware of short-term swings. Investors should know that MU is a high-beta play on memory chips and AI, and the Reddit crowd is heavily leaning long, but the stock can be volatile.

SPY.summary: SPY (S&P 500 ETF) is the second most mentioned ticker on Reddit, with 335 mentions in 24 hours, up slightly from 305. The Stocktwits sentiment is more balanced: 13 bullish vs 11 bearish tags. The conversation is mixed—some users are grateful for market gains, while others are skeptical about a "happy holiday rally." One user warns that equity inflows from long-only funds hit all-time highs in June 2026, surpassing the 2021 meme stock mania, which they see as a bearish signal. Another user notes that support held and all-time highs are still possible, but a break below 739 could trigger a flush to 720s. There's also chatter about politics (Trump vs. charities) and comparisons to Bitcoin. Overall, the mood is cautious optimism with a healthy dose of skepticism. Investors should know that SPY is a broad market proxy, and the community is divided on whether the rally has legs or if a correction is coming. The key levels to watch are 739 support and new highs above that.

MSFT.summary: Microsoft is getting a lot of love on Reddit and Stocktwits right now. The chatter is overwhelmingly bullish, with many traders expecting a bounce back to $400 or even $420 next week after what they call 'a month of hell.' The stock is ranked #3 on Reddit, and the number of mentions is steady. On Stocktwits, bullish tags outnumber bearish 14 to 4. People are calling MSFT a 'millionaire maker' alongside NVDA, META, and AVGO, and there's a belief that the recent selloff is a 'correction trap' that smart money is buying into. Some are buying calls at the close. However, there are a few cautious voices: one user mentions that Meta's Zuckerberg hinted at AI agents not going as expected, which could lead to capex cuts across big tech, including Microsoft. Another user is focused on the memory market narrative, which is more about Micron but could spill over. Overall, the sentiment is very positive, with traders expecting a strong recovery. Investors should know that the community sees MSFT as a core holding with strong AI tailwinds, but they should be aware of potential macro headwinds and capex concerns.

META.summary: Meta is seeing a lot of mixed chatter on Reddit and Stocktwits. The number of mentions dropped from 448 to 306, and the Stocktwits sentiment is nearly balanced with 12 bullish and 14 bearish tags. The conversation is dominated by concerns about a potential capex cut. One user claims 'capex cut coming soon announcement tomorrow,' which is seen as bullish by some (maybe because it could improve margins) but bearish by others who see it as a sign of slowing AI investment. There's also a lot of skepticism about Meta's hardware, like the smart glasses, with one user saying 'ain't nobody wearing them big ass glasses.' Another user warns that Meta's stock could drag down the whole sector, and there's a mention of Blackstone selling data center stakes, which could be interpreted as a negative signal for Meta's infrastructure spending. On the bullish side, some see the capex cut as a positive catalyst, and there's a colorful comment about Zuck 'gapping up' the stock. Overall, the community is divided, with a slight bearish tilt. Investors should watch for any official announcements about capex and monitor the broader tech selloff. The glasses and metaverse investments remain controversial.

WEN.summary: Wendy's (WEN) is getting a lot of love on Reddit and Stocktwits right now. The chatter is overwhelmingly bullish, with a 13:1 ratio of bullish to bearish tags on Stocktwits. People are excited about the stock's recent dip and see it as a buying opportunity. Many are talking about the 'squeeze' potential, referencing a previous run-up and subsequent pullback. The sentiment is that the stock is undervalued and due for a rebound to double digits. There's a lot of community enthusiasm, with users sharing their purchases and even planning to visit Wendy's restaurants to support the company. The recent launch of the 'Minions' meal is also a positive talking point, with fans praising the food and toys. However, some caution is warranted: the stock has seen a significant drop from its highs, and the overall market volatility could impact it. The high Reddit rank (5th) indicates strong interest, but also potential for hype-driven moves. Investors should be aware that while the community is pumped, fundamentals like earnings and same-store sales growth are key. The stock's performance may be tied to broader market trends and consumer spending. Overall, the mood is optimistic, but it's important to do your own research and not get caught up in the frenzy.

SNDK.summary: SanDisk (SNDK) is seeing a surge in Reddit mentions, doubling from 127 to 262 in 24 hours, and ranking 6th. However, the Stocktwits sentiment is mixed, with nearly equal bullish and bearish tags (15 vs 17). The discussion revolves around the stock's sharp drop, which many attribute to a market-wide 'tsunami' or manipulation. Some users are buying the dip, viewing it as a discount, while others are cautious, noting the stock got 'hammered.' There's a lot of cross-referencing with other semiconductor stocks like MU (Micron) and AMD. The sentiment is that the sector is not near its top, and SNDK could recover. However, the bearish voices are equally loud, with some calling the drop a sign of more downside. The community is divided, with some calling out 'clowns' who don't know what they're saying. Investors should note that SNDK is a well-known memory and storage company, and its stock is tied to the cyclical semiconductor industry. The recent drop may be an overreaction, but fundamentals like demand for NAND flash and competition from other players matter. The mixed sentiment suggests uncertainty, and the stock could be volatile. It's important to watch for earnings and industry trends. Overall, the community is split, with bulls seeing a buying opportunity and bears warning of further declines.

NVDA.summary: NVIDIA is getting a ton of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits right now, with mentions up slightly from the previous day. The overall vibe is very bullish, with 22 bullish tags vs only 6 bearish on Stocktwits. People are talking about AI being a massive, long-term trend—one user quoted Trump saying "AI is bigger than the Internet." There's a lot of chatter about hyperscalers spending $1 trillion on AI infrastructure by 2027, which would be nearly 3% of US GDP. That kind of spending is seen as a huge tailwind for NVIDIA, since they make the chips that power AI. Some users are dismissive of the "AI bubble" narrative, saying people will still be calling it a bubble 25 years from now. There's also discussion about memory constraints and data center bottlenecks, which could drive demand for NVIDIA's products. On the bearish side, there's mention of Michael Burry shorting Micron, which is in the same semiconductor space, and that might spook some investors. A few messages are about other stocks like IREN and RUM, but they tie back to NVIDIA as a key partner. Overall, the community is very optimistic about NVIDIA's role in the AI revolution, but investors should be aware that the stock is already highly valued and any hiccup in AI spending could lead to a sharp pullback. The sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, but the lack of critical discussion might be a red flag for contrarians.

TSLA.summary: Tesla is seeing a big jump in Reddit mentions, from 75 to 163 in just 24 hours, and the Stocktwits sentiment is strongly bullish with 15 bullish tags vs only 2 bearish. The community is buzzing about a potential gap up to $400 on Monday, with several users predicting a move back above that level. There's also discussion about short squeezes in other stocks like JACK, which might be bleeding into Tesla sentiment. Some users are comparing Tesla's sales to Rivian's, noting that Tesla sold 80k more cars than expected in Q2 while Rivian only sold 12k. That kind of comparison reinforces Tesla's dominance in the EV space. There's also chatter about crypto and AI, with one user linking Tesla to broader momentum trades. However, the messages are a bit scattered—some are just generic bullish calls or spammy promotions of other stocks. The lack of detailed fundamental discussion might mean the hype is more about short-term price action than long-term value. Investors should note that Tesla's valuation is still very high, and any disappointment in delivery numbers or margins could hit the stock hard. The community seems to be ignoring risks like competition from Chinese EV makers or regulatory hurdles. Overall, the sentiment is bullish but shallow, driven by momentum and hope rather than deep analysis.

DTE.summary: DTE Energy is getting a lot of buzz on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions nearly doubling in the last 24 hours. The sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish—no bearish tags on Stocktwits. People are excited about a partnership with Google to build a massive hyperscale data center in Michigan, which includes 2.7 GW of clean energy and a $10 million community fund. This deal is expected to save ratepayers about $1.7 billion. There's also a bullish kicker candlestick pattern forming, and CFRA reiterated a Buy rating with a price target increase to $162. Some long-term holders are sharing their stories, like one who has held since 1984. The stock is seen as a growth and income play, with diversification from its gas utility and non-utility businesses. Overall, the community is optimistic about DTE's future, driven by strong fundamentals and strategic partnerships.

AAPL.summary: Apple is seeing a surge in Reddit mentions, jumping from 12 to 101 in 24 hours, but the sentiment is mixed. On Stocktwits, bullish and bearish tags are nearly even (8 vs 7). The community is discussing a few key themes: some are bearish on Monday pre-market drops and short-term options plays, while others are bullish on Apple's strong brand loyalty, especially among youth, and its ecosystem of products and services. There's chatter about a potential folding phone, though some dismiss it as old news. A broader market rotation out of tech into legacy stocks like Apple is noted, with the Dow hitting a record close. Some users are watching for political comments from Trump that could affect tech stocks. Overall, the conversation reflects uncertainty: Apple is a solid long-term hold but faces short-term headwinds from market rotation and lack of groundbreaking innovation.

IREN.summary: IREN is getting a lot of chatter on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions jumping from 6 to 20 in 24 hours. The sentiment is mixed but leaning bullish. Many traders are excited about a potential comeback, with one user calling the run back "epic." There's also talk of a broad market rally on July 6th. However, there's a lot of frustration about the stock's history of pumping and dumping. Multiple users point out that IREN has run from $20 to $70 multiple times on hype (like AI news from Nvidia) but then crashed back down. A major negative is the CEO compensation: two executives awarded themselves $800 million in compensation, diluting shareholders by 19 million shares. One user says they're "done with this company that doesn't care about its shareholders." There's also suspicion of market manipulation, with one user claiming market makers showed their hands on June 3rd when the stock was at $70.71, with massive put options down to $40. That user predicted the drop to $40 and lower. Another user mentions a $6 billion ATM offering and CEO package as excuses to dump the stock. On the positive side, some traders are holding large positions (3,000 shares) and believe in the stock. There's also a reminder to step back and not force trades. Overall, IREN is a high-volatility play with strong opinions on both sides. Investors should be aware of the dilution risk and the pattern of hype-driven rallies followed by sharp declines. The community is split between those who see a setup for a big move and those who are burned by past losses.

AXTI.summary: AXTI has zero Reddit mentions in the last 24 hours and a low rank, but Stocktwits shows activity with 9 bullish and 22 bearish tags. The sentiment is predominantly bearish. Many users are calling it a "falling knife" and predicting further declines. One user notes that the stock is selling off faster than it rose earlier this year, potentially losing 75% of its gains in record time. There's also a mention of an AI bubble popping, grouping AXTI with other AI-related stocks. On the positive side, there is some fundamental news: AXTI signed a three-year wafer supply agreement with Coherent, including a $22.3 million prepayment. This is seen as a positive development for the company's revenue visibility. However, the market reaction seems negative, with the stock continuing to fall. Some users compare it to a $1 stock, implying it's cheap but also risky. There's also a mention of a broader market comeback on July 6th, but that's more general. Overall, the community is very bearish on AXTI, focusing on the rapid decline and potential for further downside. Investors should be cautious and consider the supply agreement as a potential catalyst, but the overwhelming sentiment is negative. The stock is seen as part of a crowded trade that is now unwinding.

COHR.summary: Coherent (COHR) is getting attention mainly due to its positioning in the photonics and AI data center space. On Stocktwits, bullish messages outnumber bearish ones 23 to 4, indicating a generally positive sentiment. The most notable positive news is a price target raise from Raymond James to $435, citing Coherent's strong position across all AI data center interconnect architectures. There's also a supply agreement with AXT (AXTI) for wafer supply, which is seen as a positive for both companies. However, the stock has been volatile recently, with a 10% drop on one day due to broader semiconductor sector weakness and reports that Meta might develop its own AI compute, which could reduce demand for external suppliers. Some traders are frustrated with the photonics sector, noting that the market seems to ignore these stocks until earnings. Overall, investors should know that Coherent is a key player in photonics for AI, but the stock is subject to sector rotations and macro headwinds. The community is cautiously optimistic, with many waiting for earnings to confirm the thesis.
